reason: The statement applies because the text describes the story of Samson, who was a Nazarite chosen by God to deliver Israel from the Philistines, fulfilling a prophetic role. Samson's birth and the prophecy surrounding it, as well as his actions later in life, align with the concept of a person prophesizing the future and fulfilling that prophecy. Despite being warned not to share his secret as a Nazarite, Samson's strength was tied to his hair, and when it was cut, he lost his power, fulfilling the prophecy of becoming weak like any other man.
